title: storage — compliance evidence
description: Storage handles user-supplied content — the most PII-heavy surface in the platform. Compliance obligations centre on: (a) data minimisation via kind-level MIME + size caps, (b) storage limitation via retention windows + tenant cascade, (c) integrity + confidentiality via signed URLs + antivirus, (d) DSAR support via cross-tenant erasure orchestration, (e) subprocessor management for the S3 / CDN / AV vendor chain.

controls.evidence: Every #[FileKind] declares allowed_mimes + max_size_mb. Uploads outside the allowlist are rejected at the storage.mime.validate middleware — the byte never reaches disk. sha256 dedup ensures we never persist the same content twice within the same tenant boundary.

Retention windows declared per kind (see retention.json) + enforced by PurgeExpiredFilesJob nightly. Tenant erasure cascades via storage::CascadeStorageErasure listener — blob refcount decremented, physical byte removed on last-referrer.
Every upload passes through the antivirus pipeline (ScanFileForVirusesJob). Infected files quarantined + never served. Every read requires a signed URL with per-issuance audit trail (SignedUrlAudit).

storage::ExportFileMetadataForSubject listener aggregates every file's metadata referencing the DSAR subject into the compliance export ZIP. Physical byte inclusion requires tenant admin approval + is handled by a separate flow.

storage::HandleDsarErasureForSubject listener iterates every File where the subject appears in owner_id / issued_to_user_id / metadata references. Emits FileErased with erasure_reason=dsar_erasure. Audit trail persists the erasure evidence for the DSAR completion report.
Third-party storage backends (AWS S3, GCS, Azure Blob), CDN vendors (CloudFront, Cloudflare), image-processor vendors (Cloudinary if used) are subprocessors. See subprocessors.json.
Every SignedUrlAudit row is a processing record. Retained 90 days minimum.

All storage backends require TLS 1.2+. S3 buckets use SSE-S3 or SSE-KMS at rest. Signed URL signatures are HMAC-SHA256. Client-Side Encryption is available for enterprise tiers requiring end-to-end confidentiality (feature-flag guarded).
FileVirusFound + StorageQuotaExceeded events feed the audit + activity + notification pipelines. Compliance module aggregates virus incidents into the daily security report.

Every file access via signed URL with issuer + TTL + audit. Tenant scope enforced at middleware layer AND at the FilePolicy AND at the disk-path prefix layer. Three defence lines against cross-tenant leaks.

S3 IAM roles rotated quarterly. Signed URL signing keys rotated per config('storage.signing_key_rotation_days'). Redis-cached signature keys expire on rotation, forcing a re-sign for in-flight sessions.
TLS everywhere. Signed URLs on HTTPS only — HTTP scheme rejected at the redemption middleware. Chunked uploads use TLS + optional client cert (mTLS for enterprise).
FilePolicy + tenant scope enforcement. `restricted` visibility files require owner or admin. Infected files hidden from every tenant-facing route.
Health probes on S3 reachability + AV scanner heartbeat + variant queue depth. Alerts on FileVirusFound + StorageQuotaExceeded + PurgeExpiredFiles cycle failure.

The `#[FileKind(allowed_mimes)]` allowlist per kind rejects arbitrary uploads. Payment-integration flows route card data through the payment provider's tokenised endpoints and NEVER through our storage module. Explicit denylist middleware refuses any file whose sniffed content matches card-number regex patterns.

controls.principle: verifiable parental consent for minor uploads
controls.evidence: When a File's owner_id resolves to a minor (via user::User.age_gate context), the upload requires a parental consent record from the compliance module. Enforced at the FilePolicy::upload level. Files uploaded by minors receive `visibility=restricted` by default.

written_to_audit_log: SignedUrlIssued (share reason only — other reasons too high-volume)
